title: ocr_result_{document_id}.json -- OCR quality + cross-validation log, one file per document v0.6
description: Per-page text extraction results, uncertain regions, and the P8 dual-path cross-validation outcome for ONE document. Produced by document-pipeline's checkpoint 1 -- one file per document (ocr_result_{document_id}.json), since checkpoint 1 runs once per document and a single shared file across documents would get silently overwritten by each subsequent document's write (write-contract replaces the whole file; see known-gaps.md's audit of this exact failure mode). v0.2 nested everything under a documents: [] array to cover multiple documents in one file -- v0.3 flattens that away since each file now covers exactly one document; document_id is a top-level field here, matching the filename. Propagation rule: if a later stage's evidence_reference quote overlaps an uncertain_region, that field is routed review_required: true. Only a per-document summary (ocr_quality, uncertain_region_count, cross_validation_status) is copied into document_manifest.json.

ocr_engine.description: Primary extraction provider/path used for this document. In the current provider-based P8 flow this is usually reader_a's provider:model label (for example 'openai-api:gpt-...' or 'claude-cli:claude-cli'); a future dedicated OCR engine should be recorded here when one is added. If the selected provider is LLM vision rather than a traditional OCR engine, record that honestly rather than implying engine-level OCR confidence exists.

vision_model_name.description: Historical field name for P8's second reader and comparator metadata. Record reader_b's provider:model label and comparator label as produced by the run. reader_b reads the same raw page blind, before it sees reader_a's output -- see cross_validation per page.

uncertain_confidence_threshold.description: Blocks below this confidence are recorded as uncertain_region. Not fixed in the schema since confidence distributions vary by engine -- record whatever value was actually used for this run. Current LLM-vision provider paths such as claude-cli/openai-api do not produce a real per-block OCR confidence signal; when no real confidence signal exists, set this to 1.0 and leave uncertain_regions empty per page rather than fabricating a number. P8's actual failure signal in that path is the agreed/disagreed cross-validation outcome, not a confidence threshold.

extraction_method.description: Whether text came from a PDF's embedded text layer or image OCR. If embedded_text, confidence-related fields may be null.

encoding_detected.description: Detected encoding for text-file inputs. Some source-cases files use CP949 -- record it.

ocr_status.description: Kept in sync with document_manifest.json's ocr_status for this document.

pages.description: Per-page results. May be empty if ocr_status is failed.

document_mean_confidence.description: Mean of per-page mean_confidence. null for embedded_text documents.

ocr_quality.description: Document-level quality summary, mirrored in document_manifest.json. Derivation (e.g. uncertain-region ratio, presence of failed pages) is an implementation detail but must stay consistent across runs; a majority of 'low' documents is a No-Go signal to check.

cross_validation_status.description: Document-level rollup of the per-page cross_validation results below. 'disagreed_pending_review' on any page means the whole document is blocked from downstream use per harness-guardrails P8 until a human resolves it. 'disagreed_resolved' is distinct from 'agreed' -- the two independent reads did NOT actually agree, a human resolved which reading was correct (see each resolved page's cross_validation.resolution) -- collapsing this into 'agreed' would misrepresent what P8 actually found. Found by actually resolving a real disagreement (CASE_012/DOC_001 page 4, I67 vs I67.8) -- there was no way to represent 'resolved' at all before this.

text_path.description: Path to this page's extracted text. data/processed/CASE_XXX/DOC_XXX/page_NNN.md -- null when cross_validation.agreement is 'disagreed': per P8, a disagreed page is never written (no tolerance threshold, no picking one reading over the other), so there is no text_path yet. Found by actually running a real document through checkpoint 1 -- a real 4-page document with 3 agreed + 1 disagreed page has no other way to represent the disagreed page's entry.

mean_confidence.description: Mean confidence for this page. null if embedded_text.

uncertain_regions.description: Blocks below threshold. Required -- omission is not allowed; an empty array means 'checked, none found', distinct from 'not checked'.

uncertain_region.description: One uncertain region. Offsets are character positions (0-based, end exclusive) within this page's text_path file. Used by later stages to check whether a quoted evidence_reference overlaps an uncertain region.

text_as_read.description: The engine's raw reading, verbatim (may be a misread).

note.description: Optional. e.g. stamp/handwriting overlap, table boundary cut off.

cross_validation_result.description: P8's dual-path cross-validation for this page: reader_a and reader_b both read the same raw page blind, without seeing each other's output, then a comparator checks material-content agreement (same names/dates/numbers/diagnoses present) rather than verbatim formatting. The two reads may be separate calls to the same provider/model or different providers; do not claim genuinely independent technologies unless the configured paths actually are independent.

vision_model_reading.description: Historical field name for reader_b's independent transcription/content summary of this page, produced before it ever saw reader_a's text.

agreement.description: This is a historical record of what the automated dual-path check found -- never rewritten to 'agreed' after a human resolution. If a human later resolves a 'disagreed' page, that fact and outcome go in 'resolution' below, not by silently changing this field (same 'never delete conflicting data' discipline as the conflict ledger, P6).

disagreement_details.description: Populated only when agreement is 'disagreed' -- what specifically differs between the two readings.

resolution.description: The usual case: set when agreement is 'disagreed' and a human has since verified which reading was correct (P8: this page's document is blocked from downstream use until this happens). null/absent while still pending. Once set, this page's text_path is written using the chosen reading's content. Also legitimately used when agreement is 'agreed' but a human found a problem compare() missed -- e.g. one reading contained fabricated content the other didn't have, which compare() (checking only for conflicting core facts, not extraneous content) can pass as 'agreed' -- found for real running CASE_012/DOC_001 page 3. In that case chosen_reading/note explain what was actually wrong and why the other reading was used instead.

chosen_reading.description: Which of the two original dual-path readings (from ocr_extract.py's reading_a/reading_b) was verified correct.

note.description: Why -- e.g. what was checked against the raw page image, or corroborating evidence from elsewhere in the case.
